Services

01
Collection organization
Arrangement, description, and cataloging of personal papers, books, photographs, and ephemera — built around your needs and goals.
02
Research assistance
Primary and secondary source research across archives, libraries, and digital collections. Thorough, efficient, and clearly documented.
03
Digitization & preservation
Planning and executing digitization projects to make physical collections accessible and ensure their long-term survival.
04
Finding aid creation
Clear, detailed finding aids that make collections navigable for researchers, donors, staff, and the general public alike.
05
Estate archiving
Sensitive, thorough handling of family papers, photographs, and objects — preserving legacies with care, expertise, and discretion.
06
Metadata & cataloging
Professional-grade cataloging using MARC, Dublin Core, DACS, and EAD for institutions and organizations of any size.
02

About

I'm a New Jersey-based archivist and librarian with an MLIS from Rutgers University and a BA in History and Literature. My work spans reference librarianship, archival processing, digitization, and metadata management, with a particular interest in the intersection of local history, gender, and community memory.

"The archive is not a neutral space — it is a choice about what gets to be remembered."

I've processed major collections at Rutgers' Archibald S. Alexander Library, including faculty papers spanning 85+ boxes and the records of the Center for Women's Global Leadership, an undertaking of over 250 boxes. I currently serve as the New Jersey Library Association's first official archivist, where I am responsible for the processing, digitization, and digital holding of the historical organization's materials. I also work as a reference and instruction librarian at Middlesex County College, teaching information literacy and offering research assistance to students of all ages.
